hoses: Supply line from hydrant to Engine 181 intake
Attack Line A: 200 ft of 1¾-inch hose from pump panel discharge to front door firefighter
Attack Line B: 250 ft of 1¾-inch hose from rear discharge to Alpha-side firefighter
nozzles: Attack Line A: ChiefXD 185 fog nozzle, 185 GPM @ 50 PSI
Attack Line B: ChiefXD 185 fog nozzle, 185 GPM @ 50 PSI
sceneElements.elevation: Grade-level residential operation, 0 PSI elevation correction

problems.title: Attack Line A Friction Loss
problems.question: Attack Line A is 200 ft of 1¾-inch hose flowing 185 GPM. Using C = 15.5, what is the friction loss in Line A?
formulaBreakdown: FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
Line A uses 1¾-inch hose, so C = 15.5.
Line A hose length = 200 ft = 2 hundreds.
problems.instructorExplanation: Attack Line A is calculated by itself from the engine discharge to the nozzle. The friction loss rounds to 105 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add nozzle pressure when the question asks only for friction loss. Friction loss is calculated before nozzle pressure is added.

problems.title: Attack Line B Friction Loss
problems.question: Attack Line B is 300 ft of 1¾-inch hose flowing 185 GPM. Using C = 15.5, what is the friction loss in Line B?
problems.instructorExplanation: Line B is longer than Line A, so it has higher friction loss and will likely govern the pump discharge pressure.
problems.explainMistake: Do not reuse the 200 ft line friction loss. Line B is 300 ft long.

problems.title: Pump Discharge Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying two attack lines. Line A is 200 ft of 1¾-inch hose flowing 185 GPM at 50 PSI. Line B is 300 ft of 1¾-inch hose flowing 185 GPM at 50 PSI. No elevation or appliance loss. What pump discharge pressure should be set for the governing line?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
When multiple attack lines are supplied, the highest pressure line governs.
problems.instructorExplanation: Engine 181 must be set to supply the highest-pressure flowing attack line. Line B requires 210 PSI, so the pump discharge pressure is 210 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not average the two line pressures. The pump must be set for the line with the highest required pressure.

problems.title: Governing Attack Line
problems.question: Line A is 200 ft of 1¾-inch hose flowing 185 GPM at 50 PSI. Line B is 300 ft of 1¾-inch hose flowing 185 GPM at 50 PSI. No elevation or appliance loss. Which attack line governs the pump discharge pressure?
problems.inputLabel: Enter 1 for Line A or 2 for Line B
problems.instructorExplanation: The longer 300 ft line has higher friction loss. Line B requires 210 PSI and governs the operation.
problems.explainMistake: Do not choose the closest or shortest line. The governing line is the line requiring the highest pump discharge pressure.

problems.title: Gauge Reading With Lower-Pressure Line Shut Down
problems.question: If the lower-pressure 200 ft attack line is shut down and the 300 ft attack line remains flowing 185 GPM at 50 PSI, with no elevation or appliance loss, what pump discharge pressure will show on the gauge?
formulaBreakdown: Line A pump discharge pressure = 155 PSI.
Line B pump discharge pressure = 210 PSI.
Line A is the lower-pressure line.
If Line A is not flowing and Line B remains flowing, Engine 181 must still supply Line B.
Gauge pressure = Line B pump discharge pressure = 210 PSI.
problems.instructorExplanation: The pump gauge displays the pressure being supplied by the pump. If the lower-pressure line is shut down but the higher-pressure line remains flowing, the pump still needs to supply 210 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not reduce the pump discharge pressure to the lower line value when that lower-pressure line is shut down. The remaining flowing line still controls the pump setting.
